text: The problem with this statement is that scoring from that spot is not the easiest thing in the world by any means. Messi is the best ever at scoring goals from this spot - centrally, just outside the box - but for your average player, and even many top players, having that much space in that spot is no guarantee of a goal. Not to mention that the pass was behind Messi and he had to adjust and move quickly before getting closed down by defenders. Messi is just that automatic. Theres a reason hes the goat.
label: hot_take

text: Portugal had a lot of issues. Mainly about how passive they were and how there was no movement. Like at all. Ronaldo isnt necessarily the issue, the man was taking up two defenders by himself. Issue was Portugal couldnt and never exposed the theoretical space Ronaldo would have created by being marked by two guys. Movement was poor and they were just slinging crosses in hoping for the best, not to mention all the side and back passing after going up early. Overall a disappointing performance. Not much else to say.
label: analysis

text: I dont mind the US per se for hosting football World Cups.. Theyre great at showmanship, football gets a reasonable amount of interest (albeit more at the youth level) and they do like big events which in turn elevates the importance of a WC even when the sport itself isnt what theyre usually into However, their current political climate does bleed heavily into the vibes of the tournament and it's def worse given how Fifa and their administration somehow manage to bring out the worst in each other. I dont think the 48 teams thing is great either because theres no hype for the group stages because its now forgiving for the big teams to a degree that they'd need to start trying to lose to go out
label: analysis

text: I keep seeing this. But do you seriously think Mexico, in its current state politically, financially, and as a sport, could legitimately host the WC even if it were only 32 teams? Look at the stadiums they selected. Azteca was newly renovated and the other two are the newest stadiums that meet the size requirements. Mexico would have had to renovate multiple stadiums, or do what Brazil did and build all new ones to replace the the others.
label: analysis

text: World Cup goal records in general are almost entirely a measure of How good your team is in general How much of a focal point you are within your team's offense The sample size just isn't large enough to provide any meaningful data outside of "oh, that's neat."
